research.product: SAP S/4HANA — Gateway OData V2 write channel (Business Partner A2X $batch with CSRF protection)

official_sources.title: Batch Processing (OData Version 2.0)
official_sources.used_for: batch POST to the $batch endpoint with Content-Type multipart/mixed and a boundary; parts require Content-Type: application/http and Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary; 'ChangeSets cannot contain retrieve requests and cannot be nested'; Content-ID referencing within a change set; 'the server must successfully process and apply all the requests in the ChangeSet or else apply none'; 202 Accepted responses mirroring the multipart structure in request order

official_sources.title: CSRF Fetching | SAP Cloud SDK (official SAP SDK docs)
official_sources.used_for: x-csrf-token header with value fetch on a HEAD pre-flight against the resource path; tokens required for non-GET requests; Set-Cookie headers of the token response must be considered for the subsequent request

official_sources.title: Use the OData v2 Type-safe Client API | SAP Cloud SDK for Java (official SAP SDK docs)
official_sources.used_for: 'the OData service sends back the CSRF token twice: once in the x-csrf-token header and once in a Set-Cookie header'; pre-flight before modification requests and manual caching/reuse of the token; 'per the specification, either all operations or none within the same changeset are successful'; per-changeset result extraction and error checking

official_sources.title: SAP Business Accelerator Hub catalog record — Business Partner (A2X), API_BUSINESS_PARTNER 1.7.0
official_sources.used_for: current API version (1.7.0), ODATA service code and service URL .../sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER, explicit batch-processing support statement for the A2X service

official_sources.title: Operations (OData Version 2.0)
official_sources.used_for: response-code conventions embedded in batch parts: 201 Created with Location for POST, 204 No Content for updates; error conditions as 4xx responses with detailed bodies

research.language_rationale: The master-data write jobs here run on .NET services; SAP documents Gateway writes as protocol-level OData 2.0 batch requests over HTTP with header-based CSRF protection, so HttpClient plus exact multipart framing per the OData 2.0 specification is the idiomatic dependency-free surface (SAP's own SDK docs describe the identical token/changeset semantics this fixture pins).

verified_facts: Modifying calls to SAP gateways are CSRF-protected: a pre-flight HEAD/GET carrying x-csrf-token: fetch returns the token in the x-csrf-token response header together with Set-Cookie session cookies, and both must accompany the write.
The token is cached and reused; SAP documents manual pre-fetch/reuse as the supported alternative to per-request pre-flights.
A rejected token yields HTTP 403 (x-csrf-token: Required); the client refreshes once and retries the same batch once.
OData 2.0 batches POST to <service root>/$batch as multipart/mixed with a boundary; every part carries Content-Type: application/http and Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary with CRLF framing.
Change sets are nested multipart/mixed parts; they cannot contain retrieve requests and cannot be nested; operations inside carry unique Content-ID values used to correlate responses.
A change set is atomic: the server applies all of its requests or none; on failure the change-set response carries a single error response that stands for every operation in it.
Successful batch responses are 202 Accepted and mirror the request structure in order: 201 Created with Location and a d-enveloped body for creates, 204 No Content for MERGE updates.
SAP gateway error documents carry error.code, error.message.value, and an innererror block (application.component_id, transactionid) that must be preserved verbatim.
API_BUSINESS_PARTNER (Business Partner A2X, version 1.7.0) under /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER explicitly supports batch processing.
